pageTitle: Neelsidhi UX Case Study: Instant Page Creation via Google Sheet Sub-Sheets

sections.title: Google Sheet → New Web Pages
sections.subtitle: Empowering our client, Neelsidhi, to create and deploy new project pages instantly by simply adding a new sub-sheet to their Google Sheet.

visual.altText: A visual representation of a Google Sheet tab transforming into a fully functional website page.

sections.title: Impact by the Numbers
sections.heading: The seamless integration of content creation and deployment drastically reduced turnaround time for new project launches.

metrics.label: Time to Deploy New Page (Old Method)
Time to Deploy New Page (New Method)

projectDescription.label: Neelsidhi Developers
projectDescription.description: A construction and real estate client who frequently launches new projects, requiring a constant stream of new, dedicated web pages for marketing and information.

projectDescription.label: The Core Problem
projectDescription.value: The client needed a scalable, zero-developer-effort solution to add new project pages (HTML pages with unique URLs) without relying on traditional CMS development or high external costs.

issues.name: High Cost of New Pages
issues.description: Each new project traditionally required developer time for manual URL creation, page templating, and deployment.

issues.name: Reliance on Developers
issues.description: The client lacked direct control over site structure and had to wait for the development team to launch every new project page.

issues.name: Inconsistent Page Structure
issues.description: Manual page creation often led to slight variations in HTML structure and inconsistent URLs.

visual.altText: A visual of new pages branching out from a central hub.

goals.name: Achieve Content Flexibility
goals.description: Allow the client's marketing team to expand the website's content and structure independently.

goals.name: Automate Page Deployment
goals.description: Make the process of adding a new project page instantaneous and entirely automated.

goals.name: Leverage Existing Pixolo Infrastructure
goals.description: Build upon the existing Pixolo Google Sheet to JSON conversion tool (Pix Sheet Data) to extend functionality to page creation.

sections.title: Extending Pix Sheet Data: New Page Creation
sections.subtitle: The Automation Logic

steps.name: Sub-Sheet Monitoring Logic
steps.description: Engineered the Pix Sheet Data API to actively scan the connected Google Sheet for the presence of any new, specially formatted sub-sheets.

steps.name: URL & Page Title Mapping
steps.description: Established a rule: the name of the new sub-sheet automatically dictates the URL slug and the main page title of the new HTML page.

steps.name: Dynamic HTML Generation
steps.description: Designed a master template that the system fills with the data from the new sub-sheet, generating a complete, unique HTML file.

steps.name: Single API Deployment
steps.description: The entire process—monitoring, conversion, and deployment—is triggered by a single API call, instantly pushing the new HTML page live on the client's server.

solutions.title: Instant HTML Page Generation
solutions.description: The feature automatically creates a new, fully functional HTML page with a unique URL whenever a client adds a new sub-sheet, eliminating developer involvement.

solutions.title: Flexible Content Control
solutions.description: The client manages their entire content ecosystem—both individual content fields and site structure—from one, familiar Google Sheet interface.

solutions.title: Zero-Cost Content Expansion
solutions.description: Once the initial setup is complete, the client can scale their website to dozens of new projects at zero additional development cost.

solutions.title: One API Call Efficiency
solutions.description: The entire complex operation is condensed into a single, efficient API process, ensuring rapid, reliable, and consistent deployment every time.

takeaways.title: CMS Functionality Without the CMS
takeaways.description: This project showcases how existing, non-traditional tools (Google Sheets) can be engineered to handle complex CMS functions, like structural expansion, for high-budget ROI.

takeaways.title: Scalability for Non-Technical Users
takeaways.description: True business scaling is achieved when non-technical staff (like the marketing team) can deploy core assets (new web pages) independently, without needing developer assistance.

takeaways.title: Simplicity Drives Adoption
takeaways.description: The success of the feature lies in its simplicity: for the client, launching a new project is literally as easy as creating a new tab in a spreadsheet.

limitations.name: Project Timeline
limitations.description: The project carried a firm deadline, pushing the team to stay aligned and deliver on time.

limitations.name: Phase 1
limitations.description: Initial planning and research took place, establishing the framework for the project.

limitations.name: Phase 2
limitations.description: Development and implementation, where the core features were built and tested.

limitations.name: Phase 3
limitations.description: Final review and adjustments, ensuring everything met the quality standards before the launch.
